headline: Fed’s Williams says monetary policy well positioned amid Middle East uncertainty
summary: Investing.com -- New York Federal Reserve President John Williams said on Monday that U.S. monetary policy is well positioned to handle economic uncertainty stemming from the war in the Middle East, though he warned that risks to both sides of the central bank's mandate have increased.

raw.headline: Fed’s Williams says monetary policy well positioned amid Middle East uncertainty

headline: Does the Iran war really matter for the U.S. economy?
summary: Investing.com -- The Iran war has so far had a limited visible impact on the U.S. economy, but Wolfe Research warns the shock is building beneath the surface, masked by a set of temporary but powerful offsets.

headline: JPMorgan Lifts Colgate Price Target to $96: Why Emerging Markets Are Powering the Defensive Trade
summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) stock picked up a fresh price target raise from JPMorgan on Monday, May 4, with the firm lifting its target to $96 from $95 while maintaining its Overweight rating. The thesis is straightforward: a higher share of sales from faster-growing emerging markets positions Colgate to outperform consumer staples peers tilted toward developed markets. ... JPMorgan Lifts Colgate Price Target to $96: Why Emerging Markets Are Powering the Defensive Trade

headline: Barclays pivots, says no Fed rate cuts in 2026
summary: Investing.com -- Barclays has abandoned its forecast for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, now expecting the central bank to remain on hold through 2026 before delivering a single 25-basis-point reduction in March 2027, as elevated energy prices slow progress on inflation.

raw.headline: Barclays pivots, says no Fed rate cuts in 2026

headline: CL Q1 Deep Dive: Emerging Markets and Cost Pressures Shape 2026 Outlook
summary: Consumer products company Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 8.4% year on year to $5.32 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.97 per share was 2.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
